channel.title: Grand Ledge Archery
channel.description: Recent content on Grand Ledge Archery

item.title: Sarah Martinez
item.description: Sarah Martinez Head Coach & Program Director Sarah has been the cornerstone of Grand Ledge Archery for over 15 years. She joined the program as a junior archer and worked her way up through every level of the sport before taking the helm as Head Coach. Under her leadership, Grand Ledge Archery has become one of Michigan’s most respected scholastic programs. Achievements 2018 National Indoor Champion — Recurve 3x Michigan State Champion (2016, 2017, 2018) USA Archery Level 4 Certified Instructor Olympic Training Center coaching certification Coached 12 individual state champions at Grand Ledge Specialties Recurve technique and form refinement Mental preparation and competition strategy Youth athlete development Advanced shooting mechanics Coaching Philosophy “Every archer has a unique natural style.

item.title: Mike Thompson
item.description: Mike Thompson Compound Bow Specialist Mike brings 20 years of competitive compound archery experience to Grand Ledge. As a former professional archer who competed at the world level, he understands what it takes to perform under pressure — and how to teach those skills to the next generation. Achievements 2015 World Field Archery Champion 5x National Field Champion Professional Archery Association (PAA) member USA Archery Level 3 Certified Instructor Equipment tuning specialist — certified by multiple bow manufacturers Specialties Compound bow setup, tuning, and customization Field archery and 3D target shooting Equipment selection for competitive archers Mental game and pre-competition routines Coaching Philosophy “Precision is the product of understanding — understanding your equipment, your body, and your mind.

item.title: Jenny Kim
item.description: Jenny Kim Youth Program Coordinator Jenny is the heart of Grand Ledge Archery’s youth development pipeline. Her patient, encouraging teaching style has introduced hundreds of young people in the Grand Ledge community to the sport — many of whom have gone on to compete at the state and national level. Achievements USA Archery Level 3 Certified Instructor Youth Program Development certification Former collegiate archer — Michigan State University 10+ years of youth coaching experience Developed Grand Ledge’s award-winning beginner curriculum Specialties Beginning archery instruction (ages 8 and up) Youth range safety and protocols Parent-child archery programs School partnership and camp programs Coaching Philosophy “Archery is one of the few sports where a kid can walk in on day one, follow the steps, and hit the target.

item.title: About Us
item.description: About Precision Archery Club Founded in 1985, Precision Archery Club has been serving the local archery community for nearly four decades. What started as a small group of enthusiasts has grown into one of the region’s premier archery facilities. Our Facilities Indoor Range 20-yard climate-controlled range 12 shooting lanes Professional lighting system Available year-round Outdoor Range 50-yard field archery course 3D target course through wooded terrain Traditional target butts at various distances Seasonal availability (March - November) Programs & Services Beginner Classes: Learn the fundamentals in a safe, supportive environment Advanced Training: Technique refinement and competitive preparation Youth Programs: After-school and summer programs for ages 8-17 Adult Leagues: Recreational and competitive leagues for all skill levels Equipment Services: Sales, repairs, and custom tuning Safety & Certification All our instructors are certified by USA Archery and maintain current safety certifications.

item.title: New Archer Workshop
item.description: New Archer Workshop Perfect for complete beginners! Learn the basics of archery in a fun, safe environment. All equipment provided. Event Details Date: January 25, 2025 Time: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM Location: Indoor Range Registration Deadline: January 22, 2025 Cost: $45 per person (includes equipment and lunch) What You’ll Learn Basic archery safety Proper stance and form Equipment overview Shooting technique fundamentals Range etiquette What’s Included Professional instruction All equipment (bow, arrows, arm guard, finger tab) Light lunch Club membership information Requirements Minimum age: 12 years old Minors must be accompanied by parent/guardian Comfortable clothing recommended Closed-toe shoes required Registration Limited to 12 participants.

item.title: Spring 3D Tournament
item.description: Spring 3D Tournament Kick off the outdoor season with our exciting 3D tournament! Navigate our challenging woodland course featuring lifelike animal targets. Event Details Date: March 15, 2025 Time: 8:00 AM - 3:00 PM Location: Outdoor 3D Course Registration Deadline: March 10, 2025 Entry Fee: $30 members, $40 non-members Course Information 30 targets through wooded terrain Distances from 10-50 yards Unmarked yardages Various shooting positions and angles Divisions Traditional: Longbow, recurve (no sights) Instinctive: Any bow, no sights or release aids Bowhunter: Compound with hunting setup Open: Any equipment allowed Schedule 8:00 AM: Registration and equipment check 9:00 AM: Safety briefing 9:30 AM: Tournament begins 2:00 PM: Scoring and awards 3:00 PM: Event concludes What to Bring Your own equipment Weather-appropriate clothing Water and snacks Insect repellent (recommended) Rain date: March 22, 2025

item.title: Winter Indoor Championship
item.description: Winter Indoor Championship Join us for our annual Winter Indoor Championship! This tournament is open to all skill levels with divisions for youth, adult, and senior archers. Event Details Date: January 18, 2025 Time: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM Location: Indoor Range Registration Deadline: January 15, 2025 Entry Fee: $25 members, $35 non-members Divisions Youth (Under 18): Recurve, Compound Adult (18-49): Recurve, Compound, Traditional Senior (50+): Recurve, Compound, Traditional Format 60 arrows at 18 meters NFAA indoor target face Awards for top 3 in each division Lunch provided for all participants Registration Contact the pro shop at (555) 123-4567 or email events@precisionarchery.

item.title: Contact Us
item.description: We’d love to hear from you — whether you’re a new archer looking to join, a parent with questions, or a competitor interested in our programs. Contact Information Grand Ledge Archery Grand Ledge High School 220 Lamson Street Grand Ledge, MI 48837 Email: info@grandledgearchery.org Head Coach: coach.martinez@grandledgearchery.org Practice Schedule Regular practices are held during the school year: Monday & Thursday: 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM (Indoor Range, GLHS) Saturday (outdoor season, April–May): 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM Join the Club New members are welcome at any time during the season.
